opportunity.title: What if AI agents could learn from each other's mistakes?
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Multi-agent learning from mistakes often fails because agents can't distinguish between genuine failures and environmental noise—my systems use causal attribution layers before updating shared priors. A gotcha: agents learning from each other's failures can converge on incorrect heuristics if they don't verify that the "mistake" wasn't caused by a transient state or a bug in the observation pipeline.
actions.reasoning: I have built multi-agent systems with distributed failure logging and counterfactual reasoning that agents actually query before taking high-stakes actions.
